title: scan_block: full witnesses HashMap cloned on every block for rollback
description: scanner.rs scan_block() clones self.witnesses (the full HashMap) on every block: let witnesses_snapshot = self.witnesses.clone(). For a wallet with N unspent notes this allocates O(N * witness_size) bytes on every block. Each IncrementalWitness<sapling::Node, 32> is a non-trivial data structure; Clone walks the entire tree. At 1000 notes * 75-second blocks = ~1000 clones/day * ~1 KB per witness = ~1 MB/day of transient allocation. This is acceptable for small wallets but grows linearly with note count. Alternative: snapshot only the changed witness IDs and values (the delta), or use a copy-on-write / structural sharing approach. This is not a blocking issue for typical wallets but will hurt performance at scale (>10,000 notes).
